conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth world magic exercise to test your application of the new rules.

Please answer using the previously mentioned Azeroth world magic rules: You are a Druid with Cenarion Circle reputation at Revered. While protecting the flag carrier in a battleground, you cast 'Wall of Thorns'. What effects will the enemies suffer respectively?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Previously mentioned Azeroth world magic rules, the effect of a Druid casting 'Wall of Thorns'</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:101] Time: 2025-07-26-09:20, Content: The user taught the casting rules of the Azeroth nature magic 'Wall of Thorns'. The prerequisites are the Druid class and Cenarion Circle reputation Revered; the core effect is creating a wall of thorns that blocks projectiles, deals nature damage to those who touch it, and reflects melee attacks, lasting for 10 seconds; applicable to battleground defense and protecting the caster from being focused. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:101] indicates that 'Wall of Thorns' can block projectiles, deal nature damage to those who touch it, and reflect melee attacks. Therefore, the Hunter's ranged attacks will be blocked, and the Warrior's melee attacks will be reflected back at them.
conversations.content: According to the rules, the Hunter's ranged attacks will be blocked by the Wall of Thorns, and the enemy Warrior's melee attacks will be reflected.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
